seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in K–2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple descriptive questions that can be tested.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions based on observations to find more information about the natural and/or designed world(s).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in K–2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple descriptive questions that can be tested.
seps.pointContent: Ask and/or identify questions that can be answered by an investigation.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in K–2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple descriptive questions that can be tested.
seps.pointContent: Define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to specifying qualitative relationships.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions about what would happen if a variable is changed.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to specifying qualitative relationships.
seps.pointContent: Identify scientific (testable) and non-scientific (non- testable) questions.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to specifying qualitative relationships.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions that can be investigated and predict reasonable outcomes based on patterns such as cause and effect relationships.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to specifying qualitative relationships.
seps.pointContent: Use prior knowledge to describe problems that can be solved.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to specifying qualitative relationships.
seps.pointContent: Define a simple design problem that can be solved through the development of an object, tool, process, or system and includes several criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to specifying relationships between variables, and clarifying arguments and models.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions that arise from careful observation of phenomena, models, or unexpected results, to clarify and/or seek additional information.; to identify and/or clarify evidence and/or the premise(s) of an argument.; to determine relationships between independent and dependent variables and relationships in models..; to clarify and/or refine a model, an explanation, or an engineering problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to specifying relationships between variables, and clarifying arguments and models.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions that require sufficient and appropriate empirical evidence to answer.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to specifying relationships between variables, and clarifying arguments and models.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions that can be investigated within the scope of the classroom, outdoor environment, and museums and other public facilities with available resources and, when appropriate, frame a hypothesis based on observations and scientific principles.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to specifying relationships between variables, and clarifying arguments and models.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions that challenge the premise(s) of an argument or the interpretation of a data set.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to specifying relationships between variables, and clarifying arguments and models.
seps.pointContent: Define a design problem that can be solved through the development of an object, tool, process or system and includes multiple criteria and constraints, including scientific knowledge that may limit possible solutions.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to formulating, refining, and evaluating empirically testable questions and design problems using models and simulations.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions that arise from careful observation of phenomena, or unexpected results, to clarify and/or seek additional information.; that arise from examining models or a theory, to clarify and/or seek additional information and relationships.; to determine relationships, including quantitative relationships, between independent and dependent variables.; to clarify and refine a model, an explanation, or an engineering problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to formulating, refining, and evaluating empirically testable questions and design problems using models and simulations.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate a question to determine if it is testable and relevant.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to formulating, refining, and evaluating empirically testable questions and design problems using models and simulations.
seps.pointContent: Ask questions that can be investigated within the scope of the school laboratory, research facilities, or field (e.g., outdoor environment) with available resources and, when appropriate, frame a hypothesis based on a model or theory.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to formulating, refining, and evaluating empirically testable questions and design problems using models and simulations.
seps.pointContent: Ask and/or evaluate questions that challenge the premise(s) of an argument, the interpretation of a data set, or the suitability of

seps.gradeBandGoal: Asking questions and defining problems in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to formulating, refining, and evaluating empirically testable questions and design problems using models and simulations.
seps.pointContent: Define a design problem that involves the development of a process or system with interacting components and criteria and constraints that may include social, technical and/or environmental considerations.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in K–2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to include using and developing models (i.e., diagram, drawing, physical replica, diorama, dramatization, or storyboard) that represent concrete events or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Distinguish between a model and the actual object, process, and/or events the model represents.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in K–2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to include using and developing models (i.e., diagram, drawing, physical replica, diorama, dramatization, or storyboard) that represent concrete events or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Compare models to identify common features and differences.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in K–2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to include using and developing models (i.e., diagram, drawing, physical replica, diorama, dramatization, or storyboard) that represent concrete events or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Develop and/or use a model to represent amounts, relationships, relative scales (bigger, smaller), and/or patterns in the natural and designed world(s).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in K–2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to include using and developing models (i.e., diagram, drawing, physical replica, diorama, dramatization, or storyboard) that represent concrete events or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Develop a simple model based on evidence to represent a proposed object or tool.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to building and revising simple models and using models to represent events and design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Identify limitations of models.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to building and revising simple models and using models to represent events and design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Collaboratively develop and/or revise a model based on evidence that shows the relationships among variables for frequent and regular occurring events.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to building and revising simple models and using models to represent events and design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Develop a model using an analogy, example, or abstract representation to describe a scientific principle or design solution.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to building and revising simple models and using models to represent events and design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Develop and/or use models to describe and/or predict phenomena.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to building and revising simple models and using models to represent events and design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Develop a diagram or simple physical prototype to convey a proposed object, tool, or process.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to building and revising simple models and using models to represent events and design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Use a model to test cause and effect relationships or interactions concerning the functioning of a natural or designed system.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to developing, using, and revising models to describe, test, and predict more abstract phenomena and design systems.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate limitations of a model for a proposed object or tool.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to developing, using, and revising models to describe, test, and predict more abstract phenomena and design systems.
seps.pointContent: Develop or modify a model—based on evidence – to match what happens if a variable or component of a system is changed.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to developing, using, and revising models to describe, test, and predict more abstract phenomena and design systems.
seps.pointContent: Use and/or develop a model of simple systems with uncertain and less predictable factors.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to developing, using, and revising models to describe, test, and predict more abstract phenomena and design systems.
seps.pointContent: Develop and/or revise a model to show the relationships among variables, including those that are not observable but predict observable phenomena.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to developing, using, and revising models to describe, test, and predict more abstract phenomena and design systems.
seps.pointContent: Develop and/or use a model to predict and/or describe phenomena.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to developing, using, and revising models to describe, test, and predict more abstract phenomena and design systems.
seps.pointContent: Develop a model to describe unobservable mechanisms.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to developing, using, and revising models to describe, test, and predict more abstract phenomena and design systems.
seps.pointContent: Develop and/or use a model to generate data to test ideas about phenomena in natural or designed systems, including those representing inputs and outputs, and those at unobservable scales.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Evaluate merits and limitations of two different models of the same proposed tool, process, mechanism, or system in order to select or revise a model that best fits the evidence or design criteria.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Design a test of a model to ascertain its reliability.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Develop, revise, and/or use a model based on evidence to illustrate and/or predict the relationships between systems or between components of a system.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Develop and/or use multiple types of models to provide mechanistic accounts and/or predict phenomena, and move flexibly between model types based on merits and limitations.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Develop a complex model that allows for manipulation and testing of a proposed process or system.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Develop and/or use a model (including mathematical and computational) to generate data to support explanations, predict phenomena, analyze systems, and/or solve problems.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: With guidance, plan and conduct an investigation in collaboration with peers (for K).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Plan and conduct an investigation collaboratively to produce data to serve as the basis for evidence to answer a question.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate different ways of observing and/or measuring a phenomenon to determine which way can answer a question.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Make observations (firsthand or from media) and/or measurements to collect data

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: that can be used to make comparisons.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Make observations (firsthand or from media) and/or measurements of a proposed object or tool or solution to determine if it solves a problem or meets a goal.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Make predictions based on prior experiences.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Plan and conduct an investigation collaboratively to produce data to serve as the basis for evidence, using fair tests in which variables are controlled and the number of trials considered.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate appropriate methods and/or tools for collecting data.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Make observations and/or measurements to produce data to serve as the basis for

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: evidence for an explanation of a phenomenon or test a design solution.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Make predictions about what would happen if a variable changes.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Test two different models of the same proposed object, tool, or process to determine which better meets criteria for success.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations in 6-8 builds on K-5 experiences and progresses to include investigations that use multiple variables and provide evidence to support explanations or solutions.
seps.pointContent: Plan an investigation individually and collaboratively, and in the design: identify independent and dependent variables and controls, what tools are needed to do the gathering, how measurements will be recorded, and how many data are needed to support a claim.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations in 6-8 builds on K-5 experiences and progresses to include investigations that use multiple variables and provide evidence to support explanations or solutions.
seps.pointContent: Conduct an investigation and/or evaluate and/or revise the experimental design to produce data to serve as the basis for evidence that meet the goals of the investigation.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations in 6-8 builds on K-5 experiences and progresses to include investigations that use multiple variables and provide evidence to support explanations or solutions.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate the accuracy of various methods for collecting data.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations in 6-8 builds on K-5 experiences and progresses to include investigations that use multiple variables and provide evidence to support explanations or solutions.
seps.pointContent: Collect data to produce data to serve as the basis for evidence to answer scientific questions or test

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations in 6-8 builds on K-5 experiences and progresses to include investigations that use multiple variables and provide evidence to support explanations or solutions.
seps.pointContent: design solutions under a range of conditions.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations in 6-8 builds on K-5 experiences and progresses to include investigations that use multiple variables and provide evidence to support explanations or solutions.
seps.pointContent: Collect data about the performance of a proposed object, tool, process, or system under a range of conditions.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Plan an investigation or test a design individually and collaboratively to produce data to serve as the basis for evidence as part of building and revising models, supporting explanations for phenomena, or testing solutions to problems. Consider possible confounding variables or effects and evaluate the investigation’s design to ensure variables are controlled.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Plan and conduct an investigation individually and collaboratively to produce data to serve as the basis for evidence, and in the design: decide on types, how much, and accuracy of data needed to produce reliable measurements and consider limitations on the precision of the data (e.g., number of trials, cost, risk, time), and refine the design accordingly.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Plan and conduct an investigation or test a design solution in a safe and ethical manner including considerations of environmental, social, and personal impacts.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Select appropriate tools to collect, record, analyze, and evaluate data.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Make directional hypotheses that specify what happens to a dependent variable when an independent variable is

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: manipulated.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Manipulate variables and collect data about a complex model of a proposed process or system to identify failure points or improve performance relative to criteria for success or other variables.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Record information (observations, thoughts, and ideas).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Use and share pictures, drawings, and/or writings of observations.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Use observations (firsthand or from media) to describe patterns and/or relationships in the natural and designed world(s) in order to answer scientific questions and solve problems.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Compare predictions (based on prior experiences) to what occurred (observable events).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Analyze data from tests of an object or tool to determine if it works as intended.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Represent data in tables and/or various graphical displays (bar graphs, pictographs, and/or pie charts) to reveal patterns that indicate relationships.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Analyze and interpret data to make sense of phenomena, using logical reasoning, mathematics, and/or computation.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Compare and contrast data collected by different groups in order to discuss similarities and differences in their findings.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Analyze data to refine a problem statement or the design of a proposed object, tool, or process.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to include investigations that control variables and provide evidence to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Use data to evaluate and refine design solutions.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Construct, analyze, and/or interpret graphical displays of data and/or large data sets to identify linear and nonlinear relationships.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Use graphical displays (e.g., maps, charts, graphs, and/or tables) of large data sets to identify temporal and spatial relationships.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Distinguish between causal and correlational relationships in data.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence for phenomena.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Apply concepts of statistics and probability (including mean, median, mode, and variability) to analyze and characterize data, using digital tools when feasible.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Consider limitations of data analysis (e.g., measurement error), and/or seek to improve precision and accuracy of data with better technological tools and methods (e.g., multiple trials).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Analyze data to define an optimal operational range for a proposed object, tool, process or system that best meets criteria for success.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Analyze data using tools, technologies, and/or models (e.g., computational, mathematical) in order to make valid and reliable scientific claims or determine an optimal design solution.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Apply concepts of statistics and probability (including determining function fits to data, slope, intercept, and correlation coefficient for linear fits) to scientific and engineering questions and problems, using digital tools when feasible.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Consider limitations of data analysis (e.g., measurement error, sample selection) when analyzing and interpreting data.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Compare and contrast various types of data sets (e.g., self- generated, archival) to examine consistency of measurements and observations.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Evaluate the impact of new data on a working explanation and/or model of a proposed process or system.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Modeling in 9–12 builds on K–8 experiences and progresses to using, synthesizing, and developing models to predict and show relationships among variables between systems and their components in the natural and designed world(s).
seps.pointContent: Analyze data to identify design features or characteristics of the components of a proposed process or system to optimize it relative to criteria for success.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Decide when to use qualitative vs. quantitative data.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Use counting and numbers to identify and describe patterns in the natural and designed world(s).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Describe, measure, and/or compare quantitative attributes of different objects and display the data using simple graphs.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Use quantitative data to compare two alternative solutions to a problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative measurements to a variety of physical properties and using computation and mathematics to analyze data and compare alternative design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Decide if qualitative or quantitative data are best to determine whether a proposed object or tool meets criteria for success.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative measurements to a variety of physical properties and using computation and mathematics to analyze data and compare alternative design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Organize simple data sets to reveal patterns that suggest relationships.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative measurements to a variety of physical properties and using computation and mathematics to analyze data and compare alternative design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Describe, measure, estimate, and/or graph quantities such as area, volume, weight, and time to address scientific and engineering questions and problems.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative measurements to a variety of physical properties and using computation and mathematics to analyze data and compare alternative design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Create and/or use graphs and/or charts generated from simple algorithms to compare alternative solutions to an engineering problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Use digital tools (e.g., computers) to analyze very large data sets for patterns and trends.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Use mathematical representations to describe and/or support scientific conclusions and design solutions.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Create algorithms (a series of ordered steps) to solve a problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.
seps.pointContent: Apply mathematical concepts and/or processes (such as ratio, rate, percent, basic operations, and simple algebra) to scientific and engineering questions and problems.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Analyzing data in 6–8 builds on K–5 experiences and progresses to extending quantitative analysis to investigations, distinguishing between correlation and causation, and basic statistical techniques of data and error analysis.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.
seps.pointContent: Construct, use, and/or present an oral and written argument supported by empirical evidence and scientific reasoning to support or refute an explanation or a model for a phenomenon or a solution to a problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. seps.pointContent: Evaluate the claims, evidence, and/or reasoning behind currently accepted explanations or solutions to determine the merits of arguments.

Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Respectfully provide and/or receive critiques on scientific arguments by probing reasoning and evidence and challenging ideas and conclusions, responding thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, and determining what additional information is required to resolve contradictions.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Construct, use, and/or present an oral and written argument or counter-arguments based on data and evidence.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Make and defend a claim based on evidence about the natural world or the effectiveness of a design solution that reflects scientific knowledge, and student-generated evidence.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate competing design solutions to a real-world problem based on scientific ideas and principles, empirical evidence, and/or logical arguments regarding relevant factors (e.g. economic, societal, environmental, ethical considerations).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Read grade-appropriate texts and/or use media to obtain scientific and/or technical information to determine patterns in and/or evidence about the natural and designed world(s).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Describe how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) support a scientific or engineering idea.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Obtain information using various texts, text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons), and other media that will be useful in answering a scientific question and/or supporting a scientific claim.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Planning and carrying out investigations to answer questions or test solutions to problems in K– 2 builds on prior experiences and progresses to simple investigations, based on fair tests, which provide data to support explanations or design solutions.
seps.pointContent: Communicate information or design ideas and/or solutions with others in oral and/or written forms using models, drawings, writing, or numbers that provide detail about scientific ideas, practices, and/or design ideas.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to the use of evidence in constructing explanations that specify variables that describe and predict phenomena and in designing multiple solutions to design problems.
seps.pointContent: Read and comprehend grade- appropriate complex texts and/or other reliable media to summarize and obtain scientific and technical ideas and describe how they are supported by evidence.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to the use of evidence in constructing explanations that specify variables that describe and predict phenomena and in designing multiple solutions to design problems.
seps.pointContent: Compare and/or combine across complex texts and/or other reliable media to support the engagement in other scientific and/or engineering practices.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to the use of evidence in constructing explanations that specify variables that describe and predict phenomena and in designing multiple solutions to design problems.
seps.pointContent: Combine information in written text with that contained in corresponding tables, diagrams, and/or charts to support the engagement in other scientific and/or engineering practices.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to the use of evidence in constructing explanations that specify variables that describe and predict phenomena and in designing multiple solutions to design problems.
seps.pointContent: Obtain and combine information from books and/or other reliable media to explain phenomena or solutions to a design problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 3–5 builds on K–2 experiences and progresses to the use of evidence in constructing explanations that specify variables that describe and predict phenomena and in designing multiple solutions to design problems.
seps.pointContent: Communicate scientific and/or technical information orally and/or in written formats, including various forms of media as well as tables, diagrams, and charts.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.
seps.pointContent: Critically read scientific texts adapted for classroom use to determine the central ideas and/or obtain scientific and/or technical information to describe patterns in and/or evidence about the natural and designed world(s).

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.
seps.pointContent: Integrate qualitative and/or quantitative scientific and/or technical information in written text with that contained in media and visual displays to clarify claims and findings.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.
seps.pointContent: Gather, read, synthesize information from multiple appropriate sources and assess the credibility, accuracy, and possible bias of each publication and methods used, and describe how they are supported or not supported by evidence.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate data, hypotheses, and/or conclusions in scientific and technical texts in light of competing information or accounts.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Constructing explanations and designing solutions in 6–8 builds on K– 5 experiences and progresses to include constructing explanations and designing solutions supported by multiple sources of evidence consistent with scientific ideas, principles, and theories.
seps.pointContent: Communicate scientific and/or technical information (e.g. about a proposed object, tool, process, system) in writing and/or through oral presentations.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Critically read scientific literature adapted for classroom use to determine the central ideas or conclusions and/or to obtain scientific and/or technical information to summarize complex evidence, concepts, processes, or information presented in a text by paraphrasing them in simpler but still accurate terms.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Compare, integrate and evaluate sources of information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words in order to address a scientific question or solve a problem.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Gather, read, and evaluate scientific and/or technical information from multiple authoritative sources, assessing the evidence and usefulness of each source.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Evaluate the validity and reliability of and/or synthesize multiple claims, methods, and/or designs that appear in scientific and technical texts or media reports, verifying the data when possible.

seps.gradeBandGoal: Mathematical and computational thinking in 9-12 builds on K-8 and experiences and progresses to using algebraic thinking and analysis, a range of linear and nonlinear functions including trigonometric functions, exponentials and logarithms, and computational tools for statistical analysis to analyze, represent, and model data. Simple computational simulations are created and used based on mathematical models of basic assumptions.
seps.pointContent: Communicate scientific and/or technical information or ideas (e.g. about phenomena and/or the process of development and the design and performance of a proposed process or system) in multiple formats (including orally, graphically, textually, and mathematically).
